title: Method and device for controlling a wastewatertank pumping system
abstract: Method and device for controlling a wastewater tank pumping system comprising a level input of the wastewater tank and one or more control pump outputs for controlling variable outflow pumps, and an electronic data processor connected to said input and outputs, and configured for controlling said outflow pumps by using probabilistic wastewater tank inflow rate and executing a machine-learning method using pre-trained reinforcement-learning policy model for controlling one or more pump outputs, and pre-training said pre-trained reinforcement-learning policy parameters by emulating the level of the wastewater tank using historical inflow data and a random initial wastewater tank level.
claims:
Claims C L A I M S 1. Device for controlling a wastewater tank pumping system of a wastewater tank having a level of wastewater, comprising, as input, the level of the wastewater tank and, as output, one or more control pump set-points for controlling variable outflow pumps, and an electronic data processor connected to said input and output, and configured for controlling said outflow pump or pumps by executing a machine-learning reinforcement learning method using: a probabilistic wastewater tank inflow rate, predicted by a time-series forecasting model based on calendar variables and historical inflow data, and a pre-trained reinforcement-learning policy model for controlling the one or more pump output set-points, wherein the machine-learning reinforcement-learning method comprises a policy method using the pre-trained reinforcement-learning policy model, said policy method comprising the use of an artificial neural network method for inputting a state of the pumping system and for outputting an outflow pump variable set-point of each pump for sampling reinforcement-learning policy actions of said model; wherein said reinforcement-learning policy model has been pre-trained by: emulating the level of the wastewater tank using wastewater tank historical inflow rate and random initial wastewater tank level, using a wastewater outflow rate model and a wastewater tank level model, with a reward function comprising optimization terms for: a wastewater tank level term for operating with the tank level within predetermined admissible level limits, and a power consumption term for decreasing power consumption of the pumping system . 2. Device according to the previous claim wherein the historical inflow data and/or the level input of the wastewater tank are sampled in irregular time periods during said pre-training of the reinforcement-learning policy model. 3. Device according to any of the previous claims wherein said pre-training comprises training said control policy using a policy gradient, alternating between sampling data through 

 interaction with the emulated wastewater tank level and optimizing a surrogate objective function using stochastic gradient ascent. 4. Device according to the previous claim wherein said pre-training of the reinforcement learning policy model is obtained by a Proximal Policy Optimization, PPO, method. 5. Device according to the previous claim wherein the PPO method comprises using a Generalized Advantage Estimation, GAE, method as advantage estimator for the Proximal Policy Optimization, PPO, method. 6. Device according to any of the previous claims wherein said policy method comprises using the artificial neural network method for outputting a probability density function of the outflow pump variable set-point of each pump unit for sampling reinforcement-learning policy actions of said model. 7. Device according to any of the previous claims wherein the wastewater tank is emulated using a state of the pumping system comprising: current wastewater tank level, pump on/off status, current wastewater tank inflow, current outflow and pump variable set- point. 8. Device according to the previous claim wherein the pre-training said reinforcement learning policy model comprises using forecasts of wastewater tank inflow for a predetermined number of time periods ahead and sampling reinforcement-learning policy actions from outflow pump variable set-points for each individual pump. 9. Device according to the previous claim wherein the forecasts of wastewater tank inflow for a predetermined number of time periods ahead are probabilistic forecasts, in particular probabilistic forecasts of 25%, 50% and 75% quantiles. 10. Device according to any of the previous claims wherein the said pre-training of the reinforcement-learning policy model further comprising a reward function comprising an optimization term for: a pump variability term for avoiding intermittent on/off pump states and repeated adjusting of pump variable speed. 11. Device according to the previous claim wherein the reward function is given by wherein r wwieveit is a positive predetermined value when the tank level is within predetermined admissible limits and is a negative predetermined value when the tank level is not within predetermined admissible limits; wherein r powert is a penalty value proportional to the power consumption of the pumping system; wherein r variaMity t is a penalty value proportional to change in outflow pump variable set-point; wherein c c 2 , c 3 are adjustable weight coefficients for each term of the reward function. 12. Method for controlling a wastewater tank pumping system of a wastewater tank having a level of wastewater, comprising, as input, the level of the wastewater tank and, as output, one or more control pump set-points for controlling variable outflow pumps, and an electronic data processor connected to said input and outputs, and said method comprising: controlling said outflow pump or pumps by executing a machine-learning reinforcement learning method using: a pre-trained reinforcement-learning policy model for controlling the one or more pump output set-points, and a probabilistic wastewater tank inflow rate, predicted by a time-series forecasting model based on calendar variables and historical inflow data, wherein the machine-learning reinforcement-learning method comprises a policy method using the pre-trained reinforcement-learning policy model, said policy method comprising the use of an artificial neural network method for inputting a state of the pumping system and for outputting an outflow pump variable set-point of each pump for sampling reinforcement-learning policy actions of said model; and previously pre-training said reinforcement-learning policy model by: emulating the level of the wastewater tank using historical wastewater tank inflow rate and random initial wastewater tank level, using a wastewater outflow rate model and a wastewater tank level model, 

 with a reward function comprising optimization terms for: a wastewater tank level term for operating with the tank level within predetermined admissible level limits, and a power consumption term for decreasing power consumption of the pumping system . 13. Non-transitory computer-readable storage medium including program instructions for implementing a device for controlling a wastewater tank pumping system, the program instructions including instructions executable by an electronic data processor to carry out the method of the previous claim.
